title: QUIC RFC 9000 DATA_BLOCKED Runtime Emission Work Item
summary: Add protected-packet proof and permanent benchmark coverage for active-runtime `DATA_BLOCKED` emission when a stream write is blocked by connection-level flow-control credit.
planned_changes:
- Add requirement-home coverage that forces connection-level flow-control exhaustion, opens the protected application packet, parses `DATA_BLOCKED`, and verifies the emitted `Maximum Data` limit.
- Add negative requirement-home coverage that keeps an ack-eliciting packet in flight and proves the duplicate `DATA_BLOCKED` signal is suppressed.
- Confirm the emitted protected advisory packet is retained by the sender/recovery ledger and can be retransmitted after loss.
- Add a permanent BenchmarkDotNet case for the stream-state helper branch that publishes connection-level `DATA_BLOCKED`.
- Add focused architecture and verification artifacts for the bounded `DATA_BLOCKED` runtime emission slice.
verification_plan: Run the focused RFC 9000 S4P1/S13P3/S19P12 requirement-home tests, run the `QuicConnectionStreamStateBenchmarks` BenchmarkDotNet suite with the Dry job, render/check the touched SpecTrace artifacts, and preserve evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-data-blocked-runtime/`.
out_of_scope: Waiting asynchronously for later flow-control credit.
completion_notes: Completed as a proof and benchmark topoff over the existing runtime `DATA_BLOCKED` branch; no production behavior changed.
